channel.title: Interior Designer on Design Moodboard Archive
channel.description: Recent content in Interior Designer on Design Moodboard Archive

item.title: Alice Lane Home
item.description:
• Useful for polished American home furnishing references across furniture, lighting, rugs, pillows, accessories, mirrors, wall art, kitchen, outdoor, and room-by-room shopping.
  • Also worth saving for its design education ecosystem: lookbook, Design Files blog, Dear Alice podcast, and home furnishing design services.
  • Potential duplicate later: Stores / Furniture / International, Stores / Objects / Home / International, Stores / Objects / Lighting / International.

item.title: BAS Stone NYC
item.description:
• Useful for stone and hard-material inspiration rather than a pure interiors studio reference.
  • The site organizes a large stone collection by material and includes “Stone in Spaces,” making it helpful for kitchens, baths, fireplace surrounds, and statement surfaces.
  • Potential duplicate later: Stores / Objects / Home / International or a future material-source category.

item.title: Blanc Marine Intérieurs
item.description:
• Good reference for interiors that balance form and function with finishes, colors, and textures meant to age well.
  • Portfolio includes residential projects and commercial work such as restaurants and the firm’s own HQ.
  • Notes to revisit: timeless detailing, texture palettes, vintage selections, and quietly refined French-Canadian interiors.

item.title: Cece Barfield Design
item.description:
• Useful for classic, decorative, and collected interiors, plus the connection between interiors and shoppable collections.
  • The site presents both design work and product/shop collections, so this may also belong in a store/object category later.
  • Notes to revisit: portfolio styling, press references, and garden-inspired collection language.

item.title: De Rosee Sa
item.description:
• Strong reference for interiors shaped around place, history, atmosphere, and human connection.
  • The studio blends architecture and interior design and also offers a small DRS Collection of pieces inspired by previous projects.
  • Potential duplicate later: Stores / Objects / Home / International for the in-house collection.

item.title: Denise Aubrey Design
item.description:
• Useful for warm, personal luxury interiors that avoid trend-first design.
  • Services span new builds, remodels, and furnishings, so save for full-home project process inspiration.
  • Notes to revisit: comfort, individuality, meaningful curation, and everyday-living-focused luxury.

item.title: Elicyon
item.description:
• Useful for high-end layered interiors where craft, narrative, precision, and global project experience matter.
  • Their portfolio spans residential, development, and commercial work, with over 125 projects referenced on the site.
  • Notes to revisit: tailored luxury, objects of desire, bespoke lighting, artful joinery, and deeply finished development interiors.

item.title: Emily Pueringer Design
item.description:
• Good reference for warm, inviting interiors that balance modern lines with an older, soulful feeling.
  • The studio works on new builds and renovations, and highlights both projects and press.
  • Notes to revisit: approachable warmth, renovation process, tile/plumbing/new-construction experience, and client-centered design guidance.

item.title: Emily Riter
item.description: From the visible Instagram profile: Emily Riter describes the account as “Home & Garden Design,” with themes around motherhood, literature, homemaking, and ownership of @heirloomterrain. Highlights include Living room, Kitchen, Garden, HeirloomTerrain, Children’s Lit, Press, Library, Recipes, and source/link collections. Save this for soft home-and-garden inspiration, family-centered homemaking, literary/home library references, kitchens, gardens, and warm living-room styling. The profile also points to heirloomterrain.com, so this may later deserve a related store/source entry if Heirloom Terrain becomes useful for products or sourcing.

item.title: Galey Alix
item.description:
• Useful as a shoppable design-reference account: projects are organized for browsing and product sourcing.
  • The site includes project shops, an outdoor/pillow/lighting/rug collection, flooring/wallpaper resources, book/story links, and gift guides.
  • Potential duplicate later: Publication & Inspiration, Stores / Objects / Lighting / International, or Stores / Objects / Textile / International.

item.title: HÁM Interiors
item.description: HÁM is useful as a reference for rooms that feel collected rather than newly decorated: old and new together, confident color, pattern, antiques, art, and natural materials. Their work spans architecture, build, joinery, interiors, decoration, and commercial creative direction, so this is a good source for studying how the shell of a room, custom details, and styling all work together. Save especially for country-house warmth, townhouses, pubs, bespoke joinery, and that “lived-in, loved, beautifully made” feeling. They also have Studio HÁM, a home and lifestyle store, so this may later deserve a duplicate store entry for furniture, lighting, accessories, art, antiques, and one-off finds.

item.title: Heather Kane
item.description: From the visible Instagram profile: Heather Kane describes the account as “homes and general hedonism” and connects it to @francesloom. Highlights include mountain house, candle launch, lighting, renovation reveals, stairs, laundry room, garden, sourcing, design, floor plans, and original tour. Save for renovation process references, relaxed home styling, lighting ideas, garden/house overlap, sourcing notes, and room-by-room project progress. Because of the Frances Loom connection, this may also be useful later as a store/source trail rather than only a designer reference.

item.title: Hoedemaker Pfeiffer
item.description: Hoedemaker Pfeiffer is a strong reference for architecture-led interiors: modern but timeless homes where renovation, new construction, and interior design are treated together. Their featured work includes projects like The Bluff, The Snug, Arrow Bay, and South Beach, which makes this useful for studying complete houses rather than isolated decorated rooms. Save for calm Pacific Northwest sensibility, architectural restraint, layered materials, and homes where the exterior, plan, and interior mood feel integrated.

item.title: House of Chais
item.description: House of Chais is useful for practical room transformation ideas: furniture matching, decor styling, fittings, soft furnishings, and pre-curated sets that make a home feel complete. The site sits between service and shopping, with personalized styling, renovation and styling, digital styling, curated furniture, fittings, and decor. Save for accessible styling formulas, cozy finishing touches, and “how do these pieces work together?” moodboard thinking. This may later deserve duplicate placement in Stores / Furniture or Stores / Objects / Home because the product-curation side is central.

item.title: Isla James
item.description: The provided website did not load safely during enrichment, so this entry is intentionally conservative. Keep it as an interior-designer reference from the import, then revisit when the site or Instagram is available to capture style notes, location, favorite rooms, project types, and whether the work leans traditional, modern, coastal, minimal, or layered.

item.title: Jean Stoffer Design
item.description: Jean Stoffer Design is a good anchor for classic, tailored interiors with a strong kitchen and cabinetry thread. The site connects the design studio to Stoffer Home, Stoffer Home Cabinetry, media, partnerships, and the book Establishing Home, so it is useful both for room references and for product/source trails. Save for timeless kitchens, built-ins, polished traditional details, and the way a designer’s studio can extend into cabinetry, retail, publishing, and media.

item.title: Jewett Farms
item.description: Jewett Farms is especially useful for custom cabinetry and craft references. Their site frames the practice around collaboration, personalized craftsmanship, and cabinetry designed for kitchens, baths, living spaces, and custom details. Save for built-in ideas, kitchen millwork, traditional craft, Maine-made cabinetry, and projects where cabinetry carries the whole room. This is another likely future duplicate under Stores / Furniture / International or Stores / Objects / Home because it is more maker/store than pure interior-designer reference.

item.title: Joanna Gaines
item.description: From the visible Instagram profile: Joanna Gaines describes herself as an “OG homebody” and “shopkeeper at heart,” linking the profile to @magnolia, @visitmagnolia, and @magnoliatable. Highlights include Sale, Shared Table, Visit Magnolia, garden, Farm Reno, Hearth & Hand, Magnolia Table, Barbie, Shark Tank, food, Mini Reni, Loloi Rugs, Fixer Upper, and Magnolia HQ. Keep this as a broad lifestyle/design ecosystem reference: warm family homes, retail and table settings, renovation storytelling, garden life, Magnolia sourcing, and accessible modern farmhouse-adjacent ideas.

item.title: Johanna van Parijs
item.description: Johanna van Parijs is more than a standard interiors entry: the site is organized around art, furniture, paintings, wood panels, prints and wood transfers, a showroom, workshops, and interior design. Save for symbolic shapes, rhythm, measurement, tropical influence, layered collage-like surfaces, and art-led interiors. This may later belong under People / Artist / International as well as interior designer, and some furniture or wall-panel references could also be useful in Stores or Objects categories.

item.title: Jojo Barr
item.description: Jojo Barr / House Nine is useful for warm, honest, informal interiors with strong spatial planning and a natural-material sensibility. The studio is London-based with projects worldwide and offers full service design, virtual design, art curation, and a store with rugs, baskets, ceramics, cushions, furniture, paint, and collaborations. Save for authentic, timeless, liveable rooms, biophilic touches, earthy texture, and examples where design service and product curation reinforce each other. This can later be duplicated into Stores because the House Nine shop is substantial.

item.title: June Walk Design
item.description: undefined

item.title: Kelsey Leigh Design Co.
item.description: Kelsey Leigh Design Co. connects directly to Heritage House, so this is a useful designer-store hybrid. The site organizes furniture, lighting, rugs, art, decor, room shopping, KLD project collections, made-to-order upholstery, and Kelsey McGregor’s vintage-inspired lighting collaboration with Alora Mood. Save for layered lived-in rooms, rustic-refined styling, warm natural light, rich color, modern European touches, and practical “shop the room” sourcing. This should probably be duplicated later into Stores / Furniture / International and Stores / Objects / Lighting / International.

item.title: Kim Lapin Interiors
item.description: Kim Lapin Interiors is a good reference for Southern California ease with real personality. Her site describes her as a Los Angeles interior designer and decorator who believes homes should reflect who people are, what they love, and how they want to live. Save for eclectic and joyful rooms, high/low mixing, vintage with showroom pieces, architectural interest, layered colors and textures, and projects ranging from residences to commercial spaces.

item.title: KLD Heritage House
item.description: This entry is the Heritage House side of the KLD world: an online shop and project archive for furniture, lighting, rugs, art, decor, made-to-order pieces, and room-by-room shopping. The site is useful for seeing how a designer’s finished projects become shoppable collections like Rustic Refined, Heritage House Cottage, Black Oak, and Cowboy Cool. Keep this for sourcing, product edits, and project-based moodboards; later it should likely live in Stores as well as People.

item.title: LA Closet Design
item.description: LA Closet Design is a focused reference for the storage side of interiors. Lisa Adams’ studio has offered full-service luxury closet design since 2007, with locations/pages for Los Angeles, NYC, Nashville, Chicago, Miami, Dallas, and Aspen. Save for closet systems, dressing rooms, storage accessories, organization, wardrobe preservation, and sleek built-in ideas that calm visual clutter. This could also become a future Stores / Objects / Home reference because the shop offers storage accessories.

item.title: Laura Hammett
item.description: Laura Hammett Living is currently more product and homeware focused than a single designer profile. The site includes homeware, furniture, lighting, wallcoverings, contemporary and modern-heritage collections, and interior design. Save for refined elegance, luxurious materials, enduring neutral palettes, burnished walnut, sculptural furniture, hand-carved oak, marble, aged leather, soft furnishings, and quiet luxury styling. This is a clear future duplicate for Stores / Furniture / International, Stores / Objects / Home / International, and Stores / Objects / Lighting / International.

item.title: Liz Mearns
item.description: undefined

item.title: Loak Design
item.description: LOAK DESIGNS is a useful Behance-based reference for architecture and interiors from Cairo, Egypt. The profile describes the studio as architecture and interior design, founded in 2017, with multidisciplinary experience across art and design and tailor-made concepts. Save for visual concept work, architecture visualization, moody project boards, product/interior intersections, and projects such as Mélange, Casa Noire, Nautilus, La Siesta, Nomad Villa, and Rewa Villa.

item.title: MALIN+LYNN
item.description: MALIN+LYNN is useful for architecture-led London residential work. The studio describes its work as contemporary, bespoke residential architecture and interior design, with a focus on unique, functional, light-filled spaces. Save for basements, extensions, mews houses, London renovations, restrained contemporary interiors, and homes where natural light and spatial transformation matter as much as decoration.

item.title: Megan Black | Home Inspo
item.description: From the visible Instagram profile: Megan Black uses the account @dropofhoneyy as “Home Inspo,” with a bio focused on beautiful homes, daily finds, sales, and a ShopLTK source page. Highlights include Shop Home, Color Palettes, This/That, Tips, Gift Guides, Dupes, DIY Cabinets, DIY Furniture, DIY Entryway, and Guess the Style. This is better treated as an inspiration/shopping source than a traditional interior designer. Save for product discovery, accessible home styling ideas, color-palette references, DIY built-in/cabinet ideas, and quick compare-and-source moodboards.

item.title: Michelle Gage Interiors
item.description: undefined

item.title: Mindy Gayer Design Co.
item.description: Mindy Gayer Design Co. is a strong everyday-living reference: tailored homes, remodels, new builds, home reveals, before-and-after posts, design tips, e-design, shopping picks, and travel inspiration. Save for calm California warmth, family-friendly rooms, approachable styling guidance, and the way the studio pairs portfolio work with helpful “get the look” and product-pick content.

item.title: Park and Oak Design
item.description: undefined

item.title: Patrick Resendiz
item.description: Patrick Resendiz is most useful as a kitchen and planning reference. His site emphasizes designing and planning projects in 3D, with services, a 3D training academy, and a design app for kitchens, bathrooms, and cabinetry. Save for kitchen layout thinking, render-to-build workflows, floor plans, cabinetry presentations, and designers who teach process as well as produce rooms.

item.title: PCD Studios
item.description: PCD Studios is a strong reference for cabinetry-led luxury interiors. Their site describes a mix of bespoke cabinetry, hand-picked antiques, modern design, craftsmanship, and intentional detail, with offices in New York and West Palm Beach. Save for rooms where cabinetry is the backbone, historical influence meets contemporary function, and traditional details are pared back into a modern-minimal result.

item.title: People / Interior Designer / Indian
item.description: Use this collection for Indian designers, studios, and rooms worth studying.

item.title: People / Interior Designer / International
item.description: Use this collection for international interior designers and studios.

item.title: Phoenix Grey
item.description: From the visible Instagram profile: Phoenix Grey presents the account as Interior Design and Architecture, with “Design Daddy” branding and a consultation path through @phoenixgreydesign. The profile is best saved for high-level interior and architecture commentary, design-direction references, and consultation-oriented design content rather than a traditional project-only portfolio. Keep as a People reference for design thinking, room critique, architectural interiors, and creator-led design education.

item.title: Sarah Solis
item.description: undefined

item.title: Shannin Williams
item.description: undefined

item.title: Sharon Hornsby
item.description: The Domino author page could not be inspected during enrichment, so this entry remains conservative. Keep it as a possible publication or design-writing source rather than a standard interior designer until the articles are reviewed. Add notes later about recurring topics, rooms, designers, styling patterns, and whether this should move to People / Publication-Inspiration.

item.title: Shauna Kelly Interiors
item.description: The website returned a protection/holding page during enrichment, so no reliable project details could be extracted. Keep this as an imported interior designer reference and revisit manually to add location, room types, palette, favorite projects, and why the work belongs in the archive.

item.title: Sitaldar
item.description: undefined

item.title: Studio Dearborn
item.description: undefined

item.title: Studio McGee
item.description: Studio McGee is a major reference for the “aspirational but attainable” American home look: warm, layered, collected, comfortable, and modern-traditional. The site frames the current aesthetic as New Heritage, rooted in classic principles and traditional craftsmanship through a modern lens. Save for full portfolio browsing, Oxford Road-type project series, paint stories, family-friendly entertaining spaces, and the link between Studio McGee design work and McGee & Co. furniture and decor. This is a clear future duplicate for Stores / Furniture and Stores / Objects / Home.

item.title: Véronique Savigné
item.description: Véronique Savigné is saved here through an Aspire feature on her Cape Town home. The article is rich with ideas: soul over polish, antiques and curiosities, U-shaped layout around a courtyard, transitional indoor-outdoor spaces, hand-knotted rugs, vintage textiles, reed ceilings, poplar beams, sun-baked clay color, African contemporary art, and rooms designed for gathering. Save for warmth, patina, texture, courtyard living, worldly collections, and homes that feel full of life.

item.title: Vivir Design
item.description: undefined

item.title: W Design Collective
item.description: W Design Collective is useful for traditional architecture studied through a current lens. Founded by Marianne Brown, the firm emphasizes enduring spaces, sound architecture, soulful home, new construction, full furnishing, technical drawings, renderings, bespoke furnishings, and accessories. W.D.C. Home adds handmade light fixtures and bespoke wallcoverings, blending past and present into everyday classics. Save for architectural design principles, character-rich interiors, project process notes, and future duplicate placement in Stores / Objects / Lighting and wallcovering/home categories.
